english: to hide from, to guard, secure, protect from, srúṅ-źiṅ sbá-ba (srung zhing sba ba) id.; to keep, preserve, sba-sri-med-par (sba sri med par) (to bestow) freely, amply, without restriction.
a veiled woman; name of a wife of Buddha Cs.
Sch. the upper part of the belly; sbó-tsil (sbo tshil) bacon C.; sbo-rkún-pa (sbo rkun pa) pickpocket C.
pf. sbos (sbos) = °bó-ba ('bo ba) 2, to swell(up), to distend, ltó-ba sbos (lto ba sbos) Lt. the belly is swollen, turgid; sbó-°krog-pa (sbo 'krog pa) Sch. 'to wheeze from inflation' (?).
v. sbág-pa (sbag pa); ráṅ-gi bú-tsai tsíg-sbóg (rang gi bu tsha'i tshig sbog) Mil., seems to imply a man that is receiving abusive language from his own sons (?).
pf. sbaṅs (sbangs), fut. sbaṅ (sbang) to steep in water, to soak, to drench; *báṅ-te bor* (bang te bor) W. soak it in water!

more frq. sbóm-po (sbom po) thick, p´ra-ba-las zlog sbóm-po (phra ba las zlog sbom po) Zam. the contrary to p´rá-ba (phra ba) is sbóm-po (sbom po); sbom-p´rá daṅ riṅ-túṅ mnyam (sbom phra dang ring thung mnyam) of equal length and thickness Dzl.; stout; coarse, clumsy, heavy, also applied to sins; sbóm-ma (sbom ma) a stout woman Cs.; sbst. thickness, stoutness, heaviness.
pf., fut. and secondary form sbar (sbar), trs. of °bár-ba ('bar ba), to light, kindle, inflame.
Wdn., n. of a tree, prob. = dbyár-pa (dbyar pa).

I. vb., pf. and imp. byin (byin), 1. to give, to bestow (in B. a common word, in W. almost unknown; yet v. smín-pa (smin pa) II.), without any ceremonial difference between high and low; to hand, deliver; to give up, deliver over; to give back, give for a present; to offer, proffer, hold out, rín-la byín-no (rin la byin no) he offered as an equivalent Pth.; ma byín-par mi lén-pa (ma byin par mi len pa) v. dgé-ba (dge ba). — 2. to add, to sum up Wdk. — II. sbst. gift, present, alms; the expression sbyín-pa γsum (sbyin pa gsum) comprises: zaṅ-zíṅ-gi (zang zing gi) the bestowing of goods, mi-°jígs-pai (mi 'jigs pa'i) the affording of protection, and c´ós-kyi sbyín-pa (chos kyi sbyin pa), the giving of moral instruction Cs., sbyín-γtóṅ (sbyin gtong) distribution of gifts, sbyín-γtóṅ c´en-po byed-pa (sbyin gtong chen po byed pa) Dzl. — sbyín-bdag (sbyin bdag) dispenser of gifts, more especially in the first beginnings of Buddhism a layman manifesting his piety by making presents to the priesthood, v. Köpp. I, 487, and in almost all legends; also the reverse, len-pa (len pa) the receiver of gifts, Dulva v. Feer Introd. p. 71. — sbyín-sreg (sbyin sreg), burnt-offering, v. Was. (194), Schl. 251 sqq.

pf. sbyaṅs (sbyangs), fut. sbyaṅ (sbyang) 1. to clean, remove by cleaning, clear away, as °dág-pa ('dag pa), esp. sdig-pa (sdig pa) Tar., sgrib(-pa) (sgrib (-pa)) Thgy.; less frq. in a physical sense, e.g. removing phlegm by vomiting Med., °k´ru-sbyóṅs ('khru sbyongs) diarrhoea Lex.; to cleanse, sbyóṅ-byed (sbyong byed) 1. cleansing, purifying, raṅ sbyoṅ-byed-kyi śes-rab (rang sbyong byed kyi shes rab) Mil. the knowledge how a man may be purified by his own doings. 2. Med.: purging medicine. — 2. to remove, take away, in a general sense Cs.; to subtract, de-rnams t´ig-mts´ams sbyaṅ-ste (de rnams thig mtshams sbyang ste) Wdk., 60 being subtracted, cf. °p´ri-ba ('phri ba); to cease, of diseases Med. — 3. to exercise, to train, blo (blo) one's mind Cs., k´a (kha) one's mouth, hence k´a-sbyáṅ (kha sbyang) eloquence Mil. (having reference also to k´a-γcáṅ (kha gcang) q.v.); sṅon yón-tan sbyáṅs-pa són-bai mt´us (sngon yon tan sbyangs pa son pa'i mthus) by dint of formerly cultivated abilities Glr.; t´ugs yóṅs-su sbyáṅs-pai skyés-bu (thugs yongs su sbyangs pa'i skyes bu) Mil. a saint of a thoroughly cultivated (or purified) mind; to exercise, to practise, da-rúṅ sbyaṅ dgos (da rung sbyang dgos) that must be practised still better; to study, sde-snód-la (sde snod la) the holy scriptures Mil., and with accus. γźúṅ-lugs (gzhung lugs) Tar. 14,9 (where byaṅ (byang) stands); rtsís-la sbyáṅ-ba (rtsis la sbyang ba) to learn mathematics Pth.; to practise, to perform; to recite, to repeat, formulas, bzlas-sbyáṅ byéd-pa (bzlas sbyang byed pa) Mil., *kor γaṅ có-c´e* (kor gang co che) W.; to accustom, familiarize, *mi daṅ* (mi dang)
